tasks.subject: [sse-resilience-connection-health] [P4] Create ConnectionItem for StatusLine
[sse-resilience-connection-health] [P4] Wire ConnectionItem into StatusLine and add chat retry button
tasks.description:
Connect the new `ConnectionItem` to the StatusLine in the session widget and add a retry button to the chat error banner for POST stream failures. ## 1. Wire ConnectionItem into StatusLine Find where `` is rendered in the widget layer. Search for ` {/* existing items */} ``` The `syncConnectionState` and `syncFailedAttempts` must be threaded from wherever `useChatSession` is called down to the StatusLine render site. This may involve adding them to a context or passing as props through the widget component tree. ## 2. Add retry button to chat error banner Find the error banner component that displays `TransportErrorInfo` in the chat UI. This is likely in `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/` or the session widget. When `error.retryable` is true and `retryMessage` is available, render a Retry button: ```tsx import { Button } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { RotateCw } from 'lucide-react'; // Inside the error banner: {error && (

{error.heading}

{error.message}

{error.retryable && lastUserMessage && ( )}
)} ``` The `lastUserMessage` should be the content of the last user message that failed. This can be derived from the messages array (last user message before the error) or stored as a ref when `executeSubmission` is called. ## 3. Thread retryMessage through `retryMessage` is returned from `useChatSession`. Thread it to wherever the error banner is rendered. If the error banner is in a separate component, pass it as a prop: ```typescript interface ChatErrorBannerProps { error: TransportErrorInfo; onRetry?: (content: string) => void; lastUserContent?: string; } ``` ## Important Notes - The ConnectionItem should be positioned after the existing items (it appears at the end of the StatusLine when visible). - The retry button uses `variant="ghost"` and `size="sm"` to be non-intrusive. - The retry button only appears when the error is retryable AND there's content to retry. - Search the codebase for the exact error banner component location before implementing. ## Acceptance Criteria - ConnectionItem appears in StatusLine when sync connection is degraded - ConnectionItem disappears when sync connection is healthy - Chat error banner shows Retry button for retryable errors - Retry button calls `retryMessage` with the last user message content - `pnpm typecheck` passes - Manually verify: disconnect network, observe StatusLine shows reconnecting, reconnect, it disappears
tasks.activeForm: Wiring ConnectionItem into StatusLine and adding chat retry button
